# Progress

## 2026-07-17

### First-Product Variant And Stock Workspace
- Source Mode: Direct user implementation request for grouped variant management and separate stock editing after multiple values exist.
- Completed: Added a compact `Variants` / `Stocks` tab workspace once more than one variant value exists, hiding the introductory variant title and description. The Variants tab groups values by label with label edit actions, value action pills, per-group add pills, and an active bottom `Add variant` action that opens and focuses the keyboard composer in one tap. Content-sized action sheets provide label edit/remove, value edit/remove/enable-disable, and stock enable-disable. Label and value editing reuse the keyboard-sticky composer with a check action, save on the action or keyboard Enter, close after save, and reject commas for single-value editing. The Stocks tab retains price, stock, and media controls, renames field labels to `Price` and `Stock`, replaces delete with a row options action, sorts explicitly or inherited-disabled rows after active rows while preserving independent generated-stock state, and owns the validation-aware final `Add item` action.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/first-product-setup-sheet.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/keyboard-inline-composer.tsx`, `apps/mobile/scripts/check-first-product-flow.mjs`.
- Brain Files Updated: `.brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`, `.brain/features/retail-ops-design-system-and-ia.md`, `.brain/progress.md`.
- Checks Run: `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:first-product-flow`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:nativewind-style`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:theme-colors`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-typechecks`; `git diff --check`; Android Expo Go interaction QA on `emulator-5554` covering the Variants/Stocks tab split, active `Add variant`, one-tap keyboard focus, group/value/stock action surfaces, inherited-disabled row ordering, comma rejection, and Enter-to-save edit behavior.

## 2026-07-16

### First-Product Inline Variant Composer
- Source Mode: Direct user implementation request with chat-input reference screenshot for inline keyboard variant entry.
- Completed: Added a reusable keyboard-sticky inline composer with horizontal pills, chat-style input, optional right submit action, disabled empty submit state, and removable selected pills. First-product `Add variant` now defaults to this inline composer behind `USE_INLINE_VARIANT_COMPOSER`, starts with filtered known variant-label suggestions, falls back to default suggestions on no match, then stays open for value entry with selected values first, common values mapped from the selected label, no plus button, comma-or-Enter entry that clears the input, and no duplicate variant-value-name row field. The composer is activated by `Add variant` and hides when the keyboard is dismissed or the user taps back into the form. Existing modal refs and modal bodies remain in code as the rollback path. The dev/preview floating theme toggle now hides while the keyboard is visible so it cannot overlap composer pills or inputs.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/keyboard-inline-composer.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/first-product-setup-sheet.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/floating-theme-toggle.tsx`, `apps/mobile/scripts/check-first-product-flow.mjs`.
- Brain Files Updated: `.brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`, `.brain/features/retail-ops-design-system-and-ia.md`, `.brain/progress.md`.
- Checks Run: `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:first-product-flow`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:nativewind-style`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:theme-colors`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-typechecks`; `git diff --check`.

### Retail Ops Product Setup Transaction Serialization
- Source Mode: Direct user error report from `retailOps.createProduct` showing `current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block` while adding an item with variants.
- Completed: Serialized product setup unit creation inside the DB transaction instead of creating product variants, inventory items, and opening-stock movements through a concurrent `Promise.all`. This avoids overlapping transaction commands on the Prisma serverless adapter and prevents the generic aborted-transaction message from masking variant setup failures during product creation.
- Changed Source Files: `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-products.ts`.
- Brain Files Updated: `.brain/progress.md`.
- Checks Run: `bun test packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-products.test.ts`; `bun --filter @ewatrade/db typecheck`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:first-product-flow`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:nativewind-style`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-typechecks`; `git diff --check`.

### First-Product Single-Step Stock Flow
- Source Mode: Direct user request with screenshot showing the now-unneeded Add stock step after variant stock was already configured.
- Completed: Removed the separate first-product stock step and converted setup to a single Add item action. For no-variant products, primary unit remains visible and price/current stock are captured inline side by side. Once at least one variant exists, primary unit, primary price, and parent stock fields are hidden and no longer required; product creation sends a hidden non-conflicting parent unit with zero stock while variant rows carry the real price and stock values.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/first-product-setup-sheet.tsx`, `apps/mobile/scripts/check-first-product-flow.mjs`.

### Mobile Login OTP Account Preflight
- Source Mode: Direct user correction after reporting that login sends OTP to a new test email and then fails verification with `No owner account exists for this email yet.`
- Completed: Hardened the mobile owner OTP request path so `login` mode checks for an existing normalized user and an available business context before creating a `Verification` row or dispatching email. The API now maps request-time auth preflight failures to `BAD_REQUEST`, and the mobile login screen keeps expected missing-account/no-business errors on the login screen instead of routing into local OTP fallback. Added focused DB query coverage proving missing accounts and accounts without business access create no login OTP, while valid existing business accounts still receive login OTPs.
- Changed Source Files: `packages/db/src/queries/mobile-auth.ts`, `packages/db/src/queries/mobile-auth.test.ts`, `apps/api/src/trpc/routers/auth.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/app/login.tsx`.

- Checks Run: `bunx biome check --write packages/db/src/queries/mobile-auth.ts`; `bunx biome check --write packages/db/src/queries/mobile-auth.test.ts`; `bunx biome check --write apps/api/src/trpc/routers/auth.ts`; `bunx biome check --write apps/mobile/src/app/login.tsx`; `bun test packages/db/src/queries/mobile-auth.test.ts`; `bun test apps/api/src/trpc/routers/auth.test.ts`; `bun --filter @ewatrade/db typecheck`; `bun --filter @ewatrade/api typecheck`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:auth-redesign`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:prompt-placeholders`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-typechecks`.

### Mobile Dry-Cleaning Service Orders
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.brain/features/business-type-onboarding-dry-cleaning.md`
- Completed: Added a production-backed mobile service-order workflow for dry-cleaning stores. Owner/admin users can create service items with SM/LG variants and update express surcharge settings; owner/admin and sales-rep users can create service orders with quantity, customer, due timing, payment status, native photo/video intake evidence or manual evidence link, and notes, review due work, and advance order status. Admin and sales-rep homes now expose Services entry points, and the workflow is registered as a protected full-screen stack modal route. Added a dev/preview-gated real-session QA route and runner that create owner/staff bearer sessions through production auth/staff tRPC flows, switch a fresh store to Dry Cleaning / Laundry, optionally seed the requested case-study services/orders, and open the workflow on Android for owner and activated staff proof.

### Marketing Onboarding E2E Proof
- Source Mode: Continued goal execution after the dashboard and marketing dev servers were restored.

### Mobile Native Google OAuth Redirect Fix
- Source Mode: Direct user report that Android Google sign-in was blocked with `Error 400: invalid_request` and `custom URI scheme is not enabled for your android client`.
- Completed: Replaced the Android/iOS Google sign-in runtime path with native Google Sign-In while keeping Expo AuthSession for Expo web only. The mobile hook now dynamically loads `@react-native-google-signin/google-signin`, configures it with the web client ID for secure ID tokens, validates Play Services on Android, handles cancellation and missing native-module fallback, and submits the returned ID token to `auth.verifyMobileGoogle`. The Expo app config now registers the Google Sign-In config plugin and derives the iOS URL scheme from the configured iOS client ID. The Google OAuth readiness guard now checks source structure so Android cannot regress to the blocked AuthSession `androidClientId` path.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-mobile-google-auth.ts`, `apps/mobile/app.config.ts`, `apps/mobile/package.json`, `apps/mobile/scripts/check-google-oauth-ready.mjs`, `bun.lock`.
- Brain Files Updated: `.brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md`, `.brain/progress.md`.
- Checks Run: `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:google-oauth-ready`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:google-oauth-fixtures`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-typechecks`; `bunx biome check --write apps/mobile/app.config.ts apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-mobile-google-auth.ts apps/mobile/scripts/check-google-oauth-ready.mjs .brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md .brain/progress.md`; `git diff --check`.
- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: Native provider smoke testing still requires a rebuilt Android/iOS development or production build; Expo Go cannot validate the native Google Sign-In module.

Admin and sales-rep homes now have real protected route surfaces at `/admin-home` and `/sales-rep-home`, with `/dashboard` acting as a role redirect. Admin users get `Home`, `Sales`, center `+`, `Stocks`, and `More` through the shared Design 01-style `MobileBottomTabs` primitive and a Design 01-style primary hero; the center `+` opens a compact Create sheet; More opens Sales Reps, Customers, Settings, and Theme, with dedicated Settings and Theme sheets; admin Home is a compact overview instead of the old long operational scroll; sales reps get a task-first home focused on session, sale, assigned stock, customers, recent sales, sync, and closeout. Oversized and keyboard-heavy workflows now route through full-screen stack modals instead of dashboard-mounted tall sheets: Sales Reps, business switching, create sale, customer book, rep clock-in, closeout, first product setup, stock intake, product links, reports, subscription, sync status, and unit conversion. ### Mobile Bottom Tabs Custom Raised Center Action

### Mobile Design System Playground
- Source Mode: `/implement-with-progress`
- Source Tickets: `.scratch/mobile-design-system-playground-implementation/issues/`
- Completed: Implemented the first mobile-only design-system playground pass for Expo with internal `/design-system` and `/design-system-pattern` routes. The playground now opens with an asset/reference foundation using provided EwaTrade app icon/splash assets, saved local design-reference images, and the Dribbble sales/order app direction before the component catalog. It catalogs semantic tokens, typography, header variants, actions, forms, list/status states, modals, bottom sheets, sticky/footer patterns, Retail Ops pattern previews, analytics KPI cards, and semantic bar-chart ready/loading/empty/error states using static sample data and shared mobile primitives. The production guard now hides the approval surface from production builds while keeping it available in development review.
- Follow-Up: Added a dev/preview-only icon shortcut on the login screen that opens `/design-system`, making the approval surface reachable from the emulator login page without exposing it to production customer workflows.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/app/design-system.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/app/design-system-pattern.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/design-system-playground/`, `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/index.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/app/_layout.tsx`, `apps/mobile/scripts/check-design-system-playground.mjs`, `apps/mobile/scripts/run-mvp-source-qa.mjs`, `apps/mobile/package.json`, `.scratch/wayfinder-mobile-design-system-playground/`, `.scratch/mobile-design-system-playground-implementation/issues/`.

### Mobile Shared Input And Button Unification
- Source Mode: Direct User Request
- Completed: Reused the login/sign-up input and button feel across the shared mobile primitives. `FormField` now renders auth-style rounded input shells for auth, filled, and line variants, with active/error borders, helper/error copy, optional leading/trailing icons, multiline support, and accessibility labels. `QuantityStepper` now uses the same rounded active-border shell for its center numeric input while preserving plus/minus controls. `ActionButton` now carries the auth-style full-width CTA base, delegates through the shared haptic `Button`, supports leading/trailing icons, and exposes `isLoading` plus `loadingLabel`. `AuthActionButton` delegates to `ActionButton`, and the floating theme toggle now uses the shared haptic `Pressable`.

### Mobile UI Redesign Wayfinder Charted
- Source Mode: Direct User Request
- Completed: Downloaded five Pinterest mobile UI reference images into `.scratch/wayfinder-mobile-ui-redesign/assets/reference-pins/`, wrote a visual reference study, and charted a local Wayfinder map for a full screen-by-screen EwaTrade mobile UI redesign. The map preserves the requested clean mobile direction while keeping Expo, NativeWind, keyboard-safe forms, floating bottom sheets, reusable components, and dark/light mode constraints visible for later implementation planning.

- Source Mode: Goal Continuation

- Completed: Tightened completed hands-on evidence validation so production evidence with remaining blockers must reference a live blocker report generated from the production env target. - Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `apps/mobile/scripts/check-shared-link-preview-url.mjs`; `apps/mobile/scripts/check-shared-link-preview-url-fixtures.mjs`
- Completed: Tightened the non-mutating shared-link public preview release proof. `qa:shared-link-preview` now requires `SHARED_LINK_PREVIEW_EVIDENCE_PATH` to be an absolute `.json` path and writes a value-safe JSON summary after fetching the deployed product link with the WhatsApp-style user agent, verifying public URL shape, checkout HTML hooks, canonical/Open Graph/Twitter metadata, image alt text, business/site context, and preview image fetches. The evidence summary records safe origin/host/path, metadata booleans, image-fetch proof, expected-name presence flags, and user agent without storing the share token or full private URL. The fixture now proves the evidence file is created, non-empty, includes metadata proof, and does not leak the share token or full URL; setup failures now aggregate missing preview URL plus missing evidence path.

### Mobile Retail Ops Share Links Phase 331 - Safe Business Host Selection
- Source Mode: Goal Continuation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/09-product-share-link-generation-and-management.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-share-links.ts`
- Completed: Hardened generated product share-link business-host selection. Stored storefront hostnames now produce share URLs only when they normalize to HTTP/HTTPS and are not localhost, IP, or local-device hosts. Unsafe primary custom hostnames are skipped so the next safe business hostname can be used; when all stored hostnames are unsafe, the URL builder falls back to the generated tenant storefront subdomain before the configured public base URL. Added DB tests for unsafe custom-host skipping and generated tenant-subdomain fallback, and updated `qa:product-share-management` to guard the safe business-host markers.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 327 - Android Production Readiness Narrowing
- Source Mode: Goal Continuation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `apps/mobile/scripts/check-google-oauth-live-ready.mjs`; `apps/mobile/scripts/run-mvp-live-readiness.mjs`; `apps/mobile/scripts/run-mvp-readiness.mjs`
- Completed: Recovered Android readiness proof and narrowed the full production readiness gate to the expected external blockers. `Pixel_3a_API_34` was started headlessly, ADB saw `emulator-5554` as an online device, and `qa:android-ready` passed. The first `qa:mvp-readiness:prod` run exposed fixture/env leakage: the Google live fixture could inherit a parent `GOOGLE_OAUTH_READY_ENV_FILE`, and aggregate readiness runners could let inherited child env files override their selected env file. `check-google-oauth-live-ready.mjs` now runs its nested base OAuth check against its selected live env file, while `run-mvp-live-readiness.mjs` and `run-mvp-readiness.mjs` pass their selected env file to child live gates unconditionally. After the fix, `qa:mvp-readiness:prod` passed source QA, workspace typechecks, MVP contracts, Android readiness, and base Google OAuth readiness before failing only on Google live API readiness, shared-link live readiness, shared-link public preview, and shared-link browser checkout readiness. The emulator was shut down cleanly after verification.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/scripts/check-google-oauth-live-ready.mjs`, `apps/mobile/scripts/run-mvp-live-readiness.mjs`, `apps/mobile/scripts/run-mvp-readiness.mjs`.
- Brain Files Updated: `brain/progress.md`, `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`.
- Checks Run: `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:android-ready` (with ADB access to `emulator-5554`); `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-readiness:prod` (first run exposed fixture/env leakage); `bunx biome check --write apps/mobile/scripts/check-google-oauth-live-ready.mjs apps/mobile/scripts/check-google-oauth-live-fixtures.mjs apps/mobile/scripts/run-mvp-live-readiness.mjs apps/mobile/scripts/run-mvp-readiness.mjs apps/mobile/scripts/check-mvp-live-readiness-fixtures.mjs`; `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:google-oauth-live-fixtures`; `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-live-readiness-fixtures`; `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-contracts`; `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:mvp-readiness:prod` (expected external live-value failure only); ADB emulator shutdown and empty-device verification.
- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: Full MVP release closure still needs externally supplied live proof values and evidence for Google live API verification, shared-link live write/email, deployed public preview, browser checkout, and the full hands-on device smoke run.

Share-link permissions now state that the web checkout resolves first-time registration and returning login through Better Auth, forwards platform customer account ids into pending order requests, reserves selected stock, audits customer/account identity into the customer book, enqueues and audits customer/merchant notifications, and supports protected follow-up completion or cancellation with payment plus pickup/delivery outcomes. The endpoint roadmap no longer lists shared-link customer account completion as a remaining MVP implementation gap; it now scopes future work to richer customer onboarding beyond shared-link checkout and provider-native payment/delivery extensions.

- Completed: Added a richer generated-link analytics panel to the mobile Product links sheet using the existing protected `retailOps.productShareLinkAnalytics` tRPC surface. The sheet now refreshes detailed analytics alongside production link creation, deactivation, and shared-link order follow-up, and displays the current analytics range/source, unique visitors, order requests, completed/cancelled requests, reserved/consumed/released quantities, revenue, and top-performing production links. The product-share source guard now protects the analytics query and panel markers so generated-link analytics do not regress back to only list-level counters.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 293 - Stock Adjustment Direction Guard
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/07-offline-sale-and-inventory-sync-flow.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Hardened manual stock adjustment entry so mobile local/offline behavior matches the production stock-operation contract. Damage and loss adjustments now always resolve to a stock decrease, found-stock adjustments always resolve to a stock increase, and correction adjustments remain user-selectable. The stock intake sheet disables invalid reason/direction combinations, explains the rule inline, and sends the normalized direction to production tRPC or the local queue. The local Retail Ops store also normalizes at the queue boundary so stale/offline payloads produce production-safe signed movement quantities before reconnect sync.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/components/mobile/stock-intake-sheet.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/store/retailOpsStore.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/store/retailOpsStore.test.ts`, `apps/mobile/scripts/check-inventory-operations-flow.mjs`.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 292 - Floating Bottom Sheet Guard Hardening
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Hardened the queued GND-style floating bottom-sheet requirement across the mobile MVP. The shared `Modal` primitive already defaulted every feature sheet to detached floating presentation; this slice added theme-derived iOS shadow styling to the floating container and strengthened `qa:mvp-surfaces` so every `*-sheet.tsx` feature sheet must import the shared modal primitive, must not render raw `BottomSheetModal` JSX, and must not opt out with `detached={false}`. The guard still allows `BottomSheetModal` as a TypeScript ref type and allows bottom-sheet virtualized list primitives inside the shared floating modal.

The share-link repository now prefers verified custom storefront hostnames, then stored tenant storefront hostnames, then derives a tenant storefront subdomain from the configured public storefront base URL, while preserving localhost/IP fallback behavior for local development. ### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 290 - First Product Description For Shared Previews
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/02-first-product-setup-wizard.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/10-shared-product-web-order-flow.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Closed the remaining metadata gap between first-product setup and rich shared-link previews by adding an optional product description to the mobile first-product sheet. The field stays compact and optional, uses prompt-style copy, enforces the API's 1,000-character description bound before continuing to stock, and explains that the copy appears on the shared product page and link preview. Production product creation now receives the description, local fallback products persist it, and queued offline `product_setup` replay includes it so synced products can produce richer storefront descriptions. Focused tests now prove API description normalization, local store persistence, and offline sync payload replay.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 288 - First Product Image URL Capture
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/02-first-product-setup-wizard.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/10-shared-product-web-order-flow.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Added optional first-product image link capture so the mobile setup can provide a real image source for product share-link previews without making the form bulky or required. The first-product sheet now accepts an optional public HTTP(S) product image link, validates it inline, includes it in production product creation, stores it in local fallback product state, and replays it through queued offline `product_setup` sync events. The API schema now accepts optional `imageUrl`, protected product creation and sync replay pass it through, and product creation stores it under `Product.metadata.retailOps.imageUrl`. This feeds the shared-link metadata fallback added in the previous phase so WhatsApp/Open Graph/Twitter previews can use the real item image when a public URL is configured.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 287 - Shared-Link Product Image Metadata Fallback
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/10-shared-product-web-order-flow.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Reduced the WhatsApp/social-preview caveat for product share links by making the shared-product response and storefront metadata image-aware. Shared product lookup now exposes `product.imageUrl` from structured product metadata keys and image/media arrays, covering both durable share-link rows and the metadata-backed fallback path. The storefront metadata now prefers a real public product image URL for Open Graph/Twitter previews when present, and falls back to the generated 1200x630 `/api/og/shared-product` card for missing, relative, local, or unsafe image URLs. The deployed preview validator now accepts either generated social cards or public product/CDN images, while still validating generated-card product/business query metadata when that route is used.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 286 - Google Live Readiness Base OAuth Composition

- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Tightened the standalone Google OAuth live API readiness gate so it now composes the base Google OAuth readiness check before allowing live provider/API QA. `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:google-oauth-live-ready` no longer validates only the live API URL, confirmation flag, ID token, mode, and expected email; it also requires the base mobile/API OAuth client IDs, audience alignment, documented env keys, and debug keystore proof covered by `qa:google-oauth-ready`. Fixture coverage now supplies synthetic Android/iOS/web client IDs and a temporary debug keystore for the happy path, and includes an explicit API audience mismatch scenario that fails through the composed base readiness gate. The live runner remains token-safe and inherits the stronger readiness guard before calling `auth.verifyMobileGoogle`.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 283 - Google OAuth Live API Gate
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Added a guarded live Google OAuth API QA path for the remaining provider proof. `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:google-oauth-live-ready` now verifies a live API URL, explicit auth-write confirmation, fresh Google ID token, login/sign-up mode, expected profile email, HTTPS/localhost safety, and disposable sign-up name/business values before any auth call. `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:google-oauth-live-run` runs the readiness gate first, then calls `auth.verifyMobileGoogle` through tRPC, verifies the returned mobile bearer token exists without printing it, checks the returned profile email and business context, and confirms the tenant/profile context matches. Fixture coverage starts a local mock tRPC API, runs the real live runner with a synthetic ID token, verifies the API request payload, and proves the runner does not print the Google ID token or returned bearer token. The live readiness bundle and live environment checklist now include this Google live API gate.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 282 - Shared-Link Browser Checkout Gate
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/10-shared-product-web-order-flow.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`

Playwright is now an explicit mobile dev dependency, and the browser fixture starts a local shared-product checkout page, launches Chromium, submits the actual runner through a POST checkout, verifies the submitted payload, and confirms the success state. `qa:mvp-live-readiness` now reports browser checkout readiness as its own live gate, and the live environment checklist includes the browser checkout setup keys without printing configured values.

- Source Mode: Goal Implementation

Production `retailOps.createProductShareLink` now selects the tenant storefront hostname when one exists, including the business subdomain, while still falling back to the configured storefront base URL. Verified custom storefront hostnames are preferred over internal subdomains, and unverified custom hostnames are not allowed to replace the internal tenant storefront link. The generated path still keeps tenant slug, store slug, product slug, and an opaque share token so existing public lookup, analytics, and deactivation behavior remain compatible.
- Changed Source Files: `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-share-links.ts`, `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-share-links.test.ts`, `brain/api/contracts.md`, `brain/workflows/retail-ops-stock-to-closeout-flow.md`, `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`, `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/09-product-share-link-generation-and-management.md`.
- Brain Files Updated: `brain/progress.md`, `brain/api/contracts.md`, `brain/workflows/retail-ops-stock-to-closeout-flow.md`, `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`, `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/09-product-share-link-generation-and-management.md`.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 257 - Android Create Sale Keyboard Fix
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`
- Completed: Continued hands-on Android Expo Go QA on `Pixel_3a_API_34` and verified dashboard, Sync status, Product links, Subscription, and Create Sale surfaces. The pass exposed that the Create Sale quantity input could be partially hidden when the numeric keyboard opened on Android. Fixed the flow by using the Gorhom bottom-sheet-aware text input path for bottom-sheet forms, keeping the selected unit row compact, hiding the duplicate selected-product section header, forwarding quantity focus/press-in events, and scrolling selected unit/customer focus through the virtualized checkout list. Retest evidence shows the quantity input and total remain visible above the Android numeric keyboard.

- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`
- Completed: Expanded the mobile theme-color guard so it now scans shared `components/ui` primitives in addition to app routes, screens, and mobile feature components. Fixed the exposed shared-control issues by moving the shared Button destructive text color to the semantic `text-destructive-foreground` token, deriving toast status palettes and shadows from active theme colors, replacing fixed black overlay tokens with semantic foreground overlays, and avoiding a false positive on the Text variant parser's `color: "default"` metadata. The action primitive guard was also made quote-style tolerant while still proving Button uses the shared haptic Pressable primitive.

### Mobile Retail Ops QA Phase 188 - Android Device Readiness Gate
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`
- Completed: Added a repeatable Android hands-on QA readiness command. `apps/mobile/scripts/check-android-qa-ready.mjs` checks the Android SDK path, confirms ADB and the emulator binary exist, verifies the expected AVD (`Pixel_3a_API_34` by default, overrideable with `ANDROID_AVD`) is installed, and requires at least one online ADB device before attempting manual Expo QA. Exposed it as `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:android-ready`.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/scripts/check-android-qa-ready.mjs`, `apps/mobile/package.json`.
- Brain Files Updated: `brain/progress.md`, `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`.
- Checks Run: `bunx biome check --write apps/mobile/scripts/check-android-qa-ready.mjs apps/mobile/package.json`; `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:android-ready`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check`.
- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: `bun run --cwd apps/mobile qa:android-ready` currently fails because no online Android device or emulator is attached through ADB. ADB and the `Pixel_3a_API_34` AVD are present. A direct emulator launch with `/Users/M1PRO/Library/Android/sdk/emulator/emulator -avd Pixel_3a_API_34 -no-snapshot -no-audio -gpu swiftshader_indirect` started the qemu process and exposed localhost ports `62810` and `62811`, but `adb devices -l` only saw those transports as `offline`; explicit `adb connect localhost:62810` and `adb connect localhost:62811` also failed. The offline transports were disconnected and the emulator session was stopped, returning ADB to an empty device list. A narrow `lsof -i -P -a -c qemu-syst` check afterward returned no qemu network process.

Wrong-audience and unverified-email checks still fail explicitly before account linking, and successful verified tokens continue into the existing mobile session bridge. The client form now keeps a controlled customer-account mode so first-time registration requires name plus email/password while returning-customer login uses email/password without confusing name requirements. Quantity entry now clamps to whole available units, submits a normalized hidden quantity, and disables checkout when no unit is available. The server action also normalizes submitted quantity to a positive whole number before creating the order request. Prompt-style placeholders were added without sample names, emails, products, or numeric examples.

### Mobile Retail Ops Staff Phase 172 - Secure Invite Link Bridge
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/04-staff-invite-and-attendant-onboarding.md`
- Completed: Added the missing secure staff invite-link bridge around the existing attendant OTP/onboarding flow. Staff invites now generate a random durable acceptance token, store only its hash, revoke prior active tokens for the same membership, and include an invite-specific URL in the staff invite notification email. The API now exposes a public `retailOps.resolveStaffInviteToken` query that returns bounded invite context without authenticating the user, while direct invite and offline-sync invite responses sanitize the secret token after notification enqueue. The mobile staff onboarding route can now open `/staff-onboarding?inviteToken=...`, show the business/email/role context before login, and route the attendant to the login screen with their invited email prefilled; completion still requires the authenticated invited staff session.

### Mobile Retail Ops Verification Phase 167 - First Product Two-Step Stock Entry
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/02-first-product-setup-wizard.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`
- Completed: Aligned the first-product onboarding sheet with the requested flow by splitting the previously single long form into a two-step wizard. New owners now enter item name, primary unit, price, and optional variants first, then continue to a dedicated current-stock step before saving. The variant helper copy no longer uses example unit names in the visible instruction text, and both production and local queued submission paths still send the same product, variant, and opening-stock payloads.

### Mobile Retail Ops Auth Phase 158 - Google ID Token Request Hardening
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/01-owner-signup-otp-and-business-entry.md`; `brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md`
- Completed: Reopened the existing `after-service` Google Cloud credentials project and rechecked the local After Service reference. After Service uses Better Auth web OAuth through a server-owned `/api/auth/*` callback with `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, while Ewatrade mobile correctly stays on Expo Auth Session plus the `auth.verifyMobileGoogle` tRPC ID-token verifier. Hardened the mobile Google hook to call Expo's explicit `Google.useIdTokenAuthRequest`, matching the server verifier contract that expects `response.params.id_token`, and cleared the submitted-token guard on verifier errors so a user can retry Google after a wrong-audience or setup failure.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-mobile-google-auth.ts`.
- Brain Files Updated: `brain/progress.md`, `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/01-owner-signup-otp-and-business-entry.md`, `brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md`.

- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: Live Google OAuth remains unverified because `.env` still has all Google OAuth client-id keys missing on 2026-07-11. The Google Cloud credentials page is open for setup; after client IDs are copied locally, Metro/API should be restarted and the login/sign-up provider flow run on a device or emulator.

- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`
- Completed: Hardened the remaining shared mobile input primitives found in the dark-mode source audit. The legacy controlled `Input` no longer hardcodes light-mode label, placeholder, border, background, text, or icon colors; it now uses semantic NativeWind classes where possible and `useColors()` for React Native-only text input and icon colors. The modern `input-2` primitive now supplies the active theme's muted placeholder color in both normal and bottom-sheet input paths when callers do not provide an override, and the reusable mobile `FormField` no longer passes a fixed placeholder color.

### Mobile Retail Ops Auth Phase 147 - Platform-Specific Google OAuth Readiness
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/01-owner-signup-otp-and-business-entry.md`; `brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md`
- Completed: Tightened the mobile Google OAuth readiness guard so native builds require platform-specific Google client IDs. Android now requires `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_ANDROID_CLIENT_ID`, iOS requires `EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_IOS_CLIENT_ID`, and web can use the web or generic client. The hook no longer fills Android/iOS client IDs from the generic value, so a missing native client falls back to the email-code path with a clear setup message instead of silently trying a web/generic audience on a native build.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-mobile-google-auth.ts`.
- Brain Files Updated: `brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md`, `brain/progress.md`, `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/01-owner-signup-otp-and-business-entry.md`.
- Checks Run: `bunx biome check --write apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-mobile-google-auth.ts`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; `git diff --check`.
- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: Did not run live Google OAuth because Google Cloud client IDs are still absent from local `.env`, and Android runtime QA is still blocked until an emulator/device attaches over ADB.

### Mobile Retail Ops Verification Phase 141 - Android Dark Mode Text Contrast Fix
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`
- Completed: Ran Android Expo Go light/dark visual QA on `Pixel_3a_API_34` using Android system night mode. The first dark-mode dashboard capture exposed a real contrast regression: screen backgrounds switched to dark through the style/theme bridge, but shared `Text` content stayed black because NativeWind CSS-variable text colors were not resolving after the system dark-mode switch. Fixed the shared mobile `Text` primitive to resolve semantic text color classes (`text-foreground`, `text-muted-foreground`, `text-primary`, destructive/success/warn variants, and common gray fixed colors) against the active theme and pass the resolved color through React Native text style. Retest confirmed the dark dashboard and Create Sale sheet now render readable headings, muted copy, banner text, product cards, quantity controls, totals, and payment controls. A patched light-mode Create Sale capture confirmed the fix did not regress light-mode readability.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/src/components/ui/text.tsx`.
- Brain Files Updated: `brain/progress.md`, `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/13-mobile-mvp-qa-and-keyboard-dark-mode-audit.md`.
- Checks Run: `bunx biome check --write apps/mobile/src/components/ui/text.tsx`; `bun --cwd apps/mobile tsc --noEmit --pretty false`; Android Expo Go light/dark route QA with screenshots under `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/screenshots/`.
- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: Ticket 13 remains in progress. Dark-mode verification has now covered the dashboard and Create Sale sheet after the text fix, but full light/dark coverage still needs the remaining auth, OTP, first-product, staff, share-link, subscription, reports/sync, and offline conflict surfaces. Larger-phone offline banner placement, broader long-list stress data, authenticated production share-link order follow-up mutation, and full MVP smoke coverage remain open.

The Retail Ops sync handler now caches `eventId` and `eventType` before exhaustive event-type narrowing so unsupported/failure responses keep the same runtime shape without strict TypeScript treating the fallback event as `never`. The shared `sum` utility now uses an explicit numeric reducer accumulator, and the Vercel utility reads environment variables through a local `globalThis.process` helper so the lightweight utils package does not require Node types.

### Mobile Retail Ops Auth Phase 131 - Google OAuth Provider Wiring
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/01-owner-signup-otp-and-business-entry.md`; `brain/api/endpoints.md`
- Completed: Added the mobile Google/Gmail provider wiring on top of the existing production mobile session bridge. The mobile app now depends on Expo Auth Session, exposes a reusable `useMobileGoogleAuth` hook, and routes login/sign-up Google buttons into the provider flow instead of the email-code fallback. The API now exposes `auth.verifyMobileGoogle`, verifies Google ID tokens through Google's tokeninfo endpoint against configured client-id audiences, rejects unverified emails or wrong audiences, and links Google accounts through the existing `Account` table before issuing the same bearer `Session` shape used by OTP. The DB auth query layer now supports verified Google identities, existing account linking, first business creation for sign-up, and returning owner login. Google OAuth env placeholders were added to `.env.example`, the existing `after-service` Google Cloud credentials project was opened for setup, and `brain/features/mobile-google-oauth-setup.md` now captures the exact project link, package/bundle ids, env mapping, SHA-1 requirement, and live QA gate.
- Changed Source Files: `apps/mobile/package.json`, `apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-mobile-google-auth.ts`, `apps/mobile/src/app/login.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/app/sign-up.tsx`, `apps/api/src/trpc/routers/auth.ts`, `packages/db/src/queries/mobile-auth.ts`, `.env.example`, `bun.lock`.

### Mobile Retail Ops Product Setup Phase 119 - Unit Template Picker Bridge
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/02-first-product-setup-wizard.md`
- Completed: Added the first mobile unit-template picker bridge to the first-product setup sheet. The sheet now reads `retailOps.unitTemplates` online, falls back to local Bag fractions and Kilogram fractions presets while offline or when production templates are unavailable, pre-fills the primary unit plus common sub-unit names, keeps unit prices manual, and sends the selected `unitTemplateKey` to `retailOps.createProduct`. Manual mode clears the template key and template-locked ratios while preserving user-entered variant rows. The variant conversion helper copy now matches the backend fractional multiplier contract. The dashboard now auto-opens the first-product setup sheet once per active empty business, using production summary inventory counts when online and local empty-inventory state when offline or production reads fail.

### Mobile Retail Ops Auth Phase 118 - Production Email OTP Bridge
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `.scratch/mobile-retail-ops-mvp/issues/01-owner-signup-otp-and-business-entry.md`; `brain/api/contracts.md`
- Completed: Added the first production-backed mobile owner email OTP bridge. The DB layer now exposes `createMobileOwnerOtp` and `verifyMobileOwnerOtp`, storing short-lived OTP hashes in the shared `Verification` table, creating or resuming verified owner users, creating the first tenant/business plus owner membership/store for sign-up when needed, and issuing a bearer `Session` token for the Expo app. The API now exposes `auth.requestMobileOwnerOtp` and `auth.verifyMobileOwnerOtp`, sends OTP emails through the shared email package, and accepts mobile bearer auth from `x-app-authorization` as well as `Authorization`. The mobile login, sign-up, and OTP screens now request and verify production OTPs first, apply the returned SecureStore session/profile, and keep local fallback behavior when production OTP calls are unavailable. At this phase, the Google/Gmail buttons still routed into the production email-code flow instead of creating fake local sessions; this was later superseded by Phase 131 Google provider wiring.

Completing a pending shared-link request with a payment method now creates a linked `Receipt`, marks the order `paymentStatus = PAID`, records `paymentState = paid_at_follow_up` plus payment/receipt metadata, still consumes the reserved stock, mirrors the completed/reservation state to durable share-link audit rows, and returns a receipt summary on shared-link order request responses. Completing without payment remains the existing follow-up-only path, and cancelling never creates a receipt.

### Retail Ops Share Links Phase 112 - Provider Delivery Receipt And Retry Audit
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `brain/database/schema.md`; `brain/api/contracts.md`
- Completed: Added the first provider delivery receipt and retry audit bridge for shared-link notifications. Email transports can now return provider receipts, email dispatch returns per-recipient sent/failed results, and the notification email service preserves recipient role, subject, provider, provider message id, and failure details. Shared-link checkout now records successful enqueue as `queued` rather than `sent`; the background notification job records actual per-recipient delivery outcomes into order metadata and durable `ProductShareLinkNotification` rows, including attempt count, max attempts, next retry timestamp for all-recipient failures, provider ids, subjects, and failure reasons. Attempts where every shared-link email fails throw back into the existing job retry loop, while partial failures are audited without resending already-successful recipients in this bridge.

### Retail Ops Stock Phase 80 - Durable Adjustment Damage Loss Bridge
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-stock-intake-and-unit-conversion-ledger.md`; `brain/api/contracts.md`
- Completed: Added the first durable stock adjustment, damage, and loss movement bridge. `retailOps.recordStockAdjustment` now rejects invalid reason/direction combinations before mutation, so damage/loss must decrease stock and found-stock must increase stock. External-id-backed adjustments can replay from durable movement rows when metadata fallback is missing, and successful adjustments mirror into one durable `InventoryMovement` row when the stock-ledger migration is available: `DAMAGE`/`LOSS` for those reasons, or `STOCK_ADJUSTMENT` for correction/found-stock, all with `STOCK_ADJUSTMENT` source, reason/source metadata, actor, note, happened-at timestamp, before/after balance, and movement group id. The API maps invalid adjustment requests to a bad-request response.

The API maps that error to a bad-request response.

### Retail Ops Stock Phase 48 - Reservation-Aware Central Decrements
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/api/contracts.md`; `brain/database/schema.md`; `brain/modules/merchant-system.md`; `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Extended the first-phase shared-link stock reservation bridge across central stock decrement paths. Staff stock assignment now checks assignable central stock as `onHandQuantity - reservedQuantity` and uses an optimistic `reservedQuantity` guard before decrementing. Unit conversion now checks unreserved source stock and guards the source decrement against concurrent reservation changes. Manual stock adjustment decreases now require unreserved stock and use the same reserved-quantity guard. This keeps pending shared-link order reservations from being consumed by assignment, conversion, or manual decrease flows before the durable reservation table cutover lands.
- Changed Source Files: `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-stock-wallets.ts`, `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-stock.ts`.
- Brain Files Updated: `brain/api/contracts.md`, `brain/database/schema.md`, `brain/modules/merchant-system.md`, `brain/progress.md`.
- Checks Run: targeted Biome check with formatter disabled for the Retail Ops stock-wallet and stock query modules; targeted Bun imports for the stock-wallet and stock query modules; marker scan for reserved-stock guards in source and Brain docs; stale current-stock wording scan; copied-source drift scan; direct whitespace scan; scoped `git diff --check`.
- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: Did not run typecheck, Prisma generate, apply migrations, dev servers, builds, database writes, stock assignment/conversion/adjustment integration tests, storefront/browser tests, or mobile/device tests because the fast Bun monorepo rule keeps this phase to scoped static verification unless heavier gates are explicitly requested. Durable `ProductShareLinkReservation` rows, durable stock movement rows for these reservation-aware changes, reservation expiry/release jobs, and admin conflict review remain future slices.

### Retail Ops Share Links Phase 47 - Inventory Reservation Bridge
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `brain/api/contracts.md`; `brain/database/schema.md`; `brain/modules/merchant-system.md`
- Completed: Added the first live inventory-reservation bridge for shared product links. Public shared-link order requests now atomically reserve the selected unit by incrementing `InventoryItem.reservedQuantity` before creating the pending `Order`/`OrderItem`, persist reservation metadata on the order and line, and keep link order counters updated in the same transaction. Protected follow-up status updates now only finalize pending shared-link requests: completing a request consumes the reservation by decrementing both `reservedQuantity` and `onHandQuantity`, while cancelling releases the reservation by decrementing only `reservedQuantity`. Central POS sale creation now checks available store stock as `onHandQuantity - reservedQuantity` and uses an optimistic `reservedQuantity` guard before decrementing stock, so in-person sales do not consume stock already held by pending product-link requests.
- Changed Source Files: `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-share-links.ts`, `packages/db/src/queries/retail-ops-sales.ts`, `apps/api/src/trpc/routers/retail-ops.ts`.

- Checks Run: targeted Biome check with formatter disabled for the Retail Ops share-link query, sales query, and API router; targeted Bun import for the Retail Ops share-link and sales query modules; targeted Bun import for the API Retail Ops router with a placeholder `DATABASE_URL`; stale reservation wording scan.
- Skipped Checks Or Unresolved Issues: Did not run typecheck, Prisma generate, apply migrations, dev servers, builds, database writes, storefront/browser tests, mobile/device tests, or live reservation/follow-up integration tests because the fast Bun monorepo rule keeps this phase to scoped static verification unless heavier gates are explicitly requested. The router import used a placeholder `DATABASE_URL` and emitted the existing Better Auth low-entropy dev-secret warning. - Completed: Added first-phase credit repayment tracking. `retailOps.recordCreditPayment` records cash, transfer, or card repayments against pending in-person Retail Ops credit sales, creates a receipt for the collected amount, stores bounded repayment events under order metadata, prevents overpayment, updates paid/balance state, and replays offline `credit_payment_recorded` events idempotently. `retailOps.creditSales` now returns paid amount, balance, last payment time, and repayment events for outstanding credit sales.

### Retail Ops Staff Stock Wallets Phase 2 - Wallet-Aware Sales
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation

- Completed: Added first wallet-aware sale deduction. `retailOps.createSale` now checks whether the acting user has assigned stock for the selected product unit; assigned-stock sales deduct the metadata-backed staff wallet and record a bounded wallet sale event while leaving central inventory unchanged, and unassigned sales continue to deduct central `InventoryItem` stock.

### Retail Ops Product Units Phase 3 - Metadata-Backed Price History
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation

- Completed: Added a first production price-history bridge for product units. Product setup now writes an initial price-history metadata entry for every unit, and `retailOps.updateProductUnitPrice` lets sales-management users change a unit's current price, append actor/previous/new price/effective time/reason metadata, reject future-dated price changes, and keep default-unit `Product.listPriceMinor` aligned while existing sale order items keep their original price snapshots.

### Retail Ops Offline Sync Phase 20 - Revoked Device Enforcement
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-saas-subscription-packaging.md`
- Completed: Added first-phase revoked-device enforcement. Offline device revocation now stores a bounded `revokedOfflineDevices` tombstone in tenant Retail Ops metadata, `retailOps.registerOfflineDevice` rejects revoked device ids, and `retailOps.syncEvents` rejects replay attempts that supply a revoked device id before processing any events.

### Retail Ops Offline Sync Phase 18 - Mobile Retry Backoff
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-07-ux-ui-mobile-starter-dashboard.md`
- Completed: Added first-phase mobile retry backoff for failed sync events. Failed events now persist a `nextRetryAt` timestamp, the production replay payload builder skips failed events until their retry window opens, the sync sheet shows the next retry time and a waiting button label, and the manual retry action clears the delay by moving events back to pending immediately.

### Retail Ops Offline Sync Phase 10 - Conflict Review Visibility
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-07-ux-ui-mobile-starter-dashboard.md`
- Completed: Added first mobile sync conflict visibility. Sync results with server error code `CONFLICT` now become local `conflict` queue items instead of ordinary failed retries, stay out of automatic replay until the operator marks them reviewed, and display as review work in the sync sheet. The dashboard banner prioritizes conflicts ahead of failed retries, and the local reports sync count includes pending, failed, and conflict work.

### Retail Ops Offline Sync Phase 9 - Failed Event Retry UX
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-07-ux-ui-mobile-starter-dashboard.md`
- Completed: Added first mobile failed-event retry visibility for offline sync. Failed sync events now retain server error code/message and failed time in the local queue, mutation-wide sync failures mark attempted events as failed with a request-level message, supported failed events are included in future replay payloads, and the sync sheet exposes "Needs retry" rows with per-event and bulk retry actions. The dashboard banner now surfaces failed sync work before the sheet is opened, and report sync counts include retryable failures.

### Retail Ops Offline Sync Phase 8 - Customer Upsert Replay
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-sales-recording-and-payment-capture.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-07-ux-ui-mobile-starter-dashboard.md`
- Completed: Added first-phase customer upsert replay to the production and mobile sync path. `retailOps.syncEvents` now supports `customer_upsert`; production validates that the referenced Retail Ops sale external id exists, then returns the derived customer-book identity instead of writing a dedicated customer row. The mobile sync helper now builds customer replay payloads from local customers plus their sale event external ids, and the local store marks applied customers synced through the normal sync resolution path.

### Retail Ops Offline Sync Phase 7 - Closeout Replay
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-end-of-day-closeout-and-reconciliation.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-07-ux-ui-mobile-starter-dashboard.md`
- Completed: Added idempotent closeout replay to the production and mobile sync path. `retailOps.closeSession` now accepts `externalId`, stores successful close-session replay results in store metadata, and returns duplicate replay results before open-session validation. `retailOps.syncEvents` now supports `closeout_created`, and the mobile sync helper stores the local rep session id on new closeouts, waits for that session's production cashier session id, builds close-session replay payloads from local closeouts, and marks applied closeouts plus their closed sessions as synced.

- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-rep-clock-in-and-opening-inventory.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-07-ux-ui-mobile-starter-dashboard.md`

### Retail Ops Offline Sync Phase 5 - Share Link Replay
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-offline-first-sales-sync.md`; `brain/plans/2026-07-07-ux-ui-mobile-starter-dashboard.md`
- Completed: Added idempotent share-link replay to the production and mobile sync path. `retailOps.createProductShareLink` and `retailOps.deactivateProductShareLink` now accept `externalId`; create replay returns the existing generated link for the same product external id, and deactivation replay returns the already-deactivated link without moving deactivation metadata. `retailOps.syncEvents` now supports `share_link_created` and `share_link_deactivated`, and the mobile sync helper builds share-link payloads only after product/link production ids exist, stores returned production link ids and URLs locally, and marks applied share-link events synced.

### Retail Ops Offline Sync API Phase 1 - Idempotent Event Replay

### Retail Ops Sale API Phase 2 - External Id Replay Idempotency

### Retail Ops Recent Sales API Phase 1 - In-Person Sale List
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-retail-ops-dashboard-reports.md`

### Retail Ops Customer Book API Phase 1 - Derived Order Read Model
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-sales-recording-and-payment-capture.md`
- Completed: Added the first production customer-book read bridge. The protected `retailOps.customerBook` procedure resolves the active tenant/store and returns searchable, bounded customer entries derived from recent non-cancelled orders. The DB helper groups customer history by email first, then phone, then normalized name, and returns display data, order count, total spend, first/last seen timestamps, and last order summary for mobile customer pickers.

### Retail Ops Product Share-Link API Phase 1 - Metadata-Backed Links And Pending Requests
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/features/mobile-retail-ops-mvp-spec.md`
- Completed: Added the first production product share-link bridge. The public `retailOps.sharedProduct` procedure resolves active shared product links by tenant/store/product slug and opaque token, records a first view count, and returns web product-page data. The public `retailOps.createSharedProductOrderRequest` procedure creates a pending order request with customer details and share-link attribution, rejects over-available quantities, and enqueues customer/merchant notification dispatch. The protected `retailOps.productShareLinks`, `retailOps.createProductShareLink`, and `retailOps.deactivateProductShareLink` procedures resolve tenant/store context, allow POS-capable roles to list and create generated product URLs, store opaque token link records in product metadata, and let creators or manager-level users deactivate links without deleting history. The storefront app now renders `/p/[tenantSlug]/[storeSlug]/[productSlug]?share=...` with server metadata, a generated preview image, variant selection, customer fields, and a pending order-request form.

### Retail Ops Session API Phase 1 - Open And Close Cashier Session
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-rep-clock-in-and-opening-inventory.md`
- Completed: Added the first production session lifecycle bridge. The protected `retailOps.openSession` procedure creates an open cashier/rep session for the acting user and selected store with duplicate-open prevention. The protected `retailOps.closeSession` procedure closes the acting user's open session, records closing cash, totals linked receipts by payment method, and returns expected cash plus cash variance.

### Retail Ops Unit Conversion API Phase 1 - Inventory Balance Transfer
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-stock-intake-and-unit-conversion-ledger.md`
- Completed: Added the first production unit-conversion mutation. The protected `retailOps.recordUnitConversion` tRPC procedure now validates source and target variants in the selected tenant/store, requires both units to belong to the same product, atomically decrements source stock when enough stock exists, increments or creates target stock, and returns conversion metadata plus before/after balances.

### Mobile Rep Clock-In Phase 1 - Local Opening Inventory
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
- Source Brain Record: `brain/plans/2026-07-06-feature-rep-clock-in-and-opening-inventory.md`
- Completed: Added a local rep clock-in and opening inventory bridge for the mobile MVP. The Retail Ops store now persists rep sessions with opening inventory snapshots, variance counts, pending sync state, sale session linkage, and closeout-driven local session closure. ### Mobile Stock Intake Phase 1 - Local Movement Ledger
- Source Mode: Goal Implementation
